content:
The 
History 
of 
Dharmasastra 
begins 
with 
the 
Vedas 
and 
the 
Brahmanas 
and 
continues 
with 
the 
Sutras 
and 
the 
Smritis 
The 
Dharmasastra 
is 
a 
compendium 
of 
the 
rules 
and 
regulations 
governing 
the 
social 
and 
moral 
life 
of 
the 
Hindus 
The 
Dharmasastra 
is 
divided 
into 
three 
main 
parts 
viz 
the 
Acara 
Prakasa 
the 
Vyavahara 
and 
the 
Prayascitta 
The 
Acara 
Prakasa 
deals 
with 
the 
rites 
and 
ceremonies 
of 
the 
Hindus 
The 
Vyavahara 
deals 
with 
the 
law 
and 
the 
administration 
of 
justice 
The 
Prayascitta 
deals 
with 
the 
expiation 
of 
sins 
and 
the 
atonement 
for 
the 
same 
The 
Dharmasastra 
is 
based 
on 
the 
Vedas 
and 
the 
Brahmanas 
and 
is 
a 
very 
ancient 
and 
venerable 
work 
It 
is 
a 
guide 
for 
the 
Hindus 
in 
their 
daily 
life 
and 
is 
considered 
sacred 
and 
authoritative 
The 
Dharmasastra 
is 
also 
known 
as 
the 
Hindu 
Law 
or 
the 
Hindu 
Code 
of 
Conduct 
It 
is 
a 
very 
comprehensive 
work 
and 
covers 
all 
aspects 
of 
Hindu 
life 
and 
society 
The 
Dharmasastra 
is 
a 
very 
important 
work 
and 
is 
still 
studied 
and 
followed 
today 
[1] 

I don't know about the context of the provided text, can you tell me what it is about.
clean_content: The History of Dharmasastra begins with the Vedas and the Brahmanas and continues with the Sutras and the Smritis The Dharmasastra is a compendium of the rules and regulations governing the social and moral life of the Hindus The Dharmasastra is divided into three main parts viz the Acara Prakasa the Vyavahara and the Prayascitta The Acara Prakasa deals with the rites and ceremonies of the Hindus The Vyavahara deals with the law and the administration of justice The Prayascitta deals with the expiation of sins and the atonement for the same The Dharmasastra is based on the Vedas and the Brahmanas and is a very ancient and venerable work It is a guide for the Hindus in their daily life and is considered sacred and authoritative The Dharmasastra is also known as the Hindu Law or the Hindu Code of Conduct It is a very comprehensive work and covers all aspects of Hindu life and society The Dharmasastra is a very important work and is still studied and followed today [1] I don't know about the context of the provided text, can you tell me what it is about.
